content:
2 min read
1 hour ago

--

For quite some time now, I have been telling myself that I will start writing when I am good at coding, when I am good at communicating, and when I finally become good enough to offer something unique to my lovely subscribers. This moment never came, and today, this day, I decided to start a series of articles that will tell you about my journey as a web developer.
Hello there, my name is Abdullahi, and I am a full-stack web developer. Most of my time debugging applications that somehow managed to run into production learning new web technologies and implementing cool concepts in my mind. Some concepts work, and most of them don’t, which brings me to my point. I want to share my journey as a web developer both the good and the bad times the debugging sessions that kept me awake at night over an insignificant semicolon The sheer frustration of thinking that I am not good enough just to give up and outwork everyone to finally achieve success and feel euphoric about it.
This is what I want to talk about, not some self-centered motivation-filled speech about how great I am at being a web developer because I am not. Everyone has to keep learning new things to level up in their career as a web developer. This series will be about my notes on web development, JavaScript, Node.js, Python, and API development in general or maybe some cool concepts about AI. It will also comprise some revelations from the web dev projects I have been working on. Some of the problems kept me awake at nights for weeks and months until I stumbled upon something that eventually made my life as a web developer easier.
If I can help even a single one of you to gain some insight, learn a new concept and break out of a mental model or just feel better about your journey as a web developer, then this will be an experience of a lifetime that I will always treasure. We are all just starting, so this is the beginning of my journey as a web developer, and I hope that you will be a part of it. Thank you so much again for your time, and let’s build something great together as web developers.
